data.title: Canadian headline inflation hits three-month high at 2.4%
data.description:
Canada’s headline inflation accelerated to 2.4% in December 2025 from 2.2% in November, marking a three-month high and coming in well above market expectations for an unchanged reading.

The pickup was driven by base effects linked to the temporary GST and HST break introduced in December 2024, which pushed up inflation for restaurant food to 8.5% from 3.3% in November, store-bought alcoholic beverages to 5.6% from 3%, and toys, games and hobby supplies to 7.5% from -0.5%, partially offset by the shelter category where inflation fell to 2.1% from 2.3% and transportation prices turned lower, falling 0.5% after rising 0.7% a month earlier.

data.title: Wipro Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 in-line, revenue of $2.62B misses by $30M
data.description:
• Wipro press release (WIT): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 in-line.
  • Revenue of $2.62B (+0.4% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Gross revenue at Rs 235.6 billion ($2,622.0 million), increase of 3.8% QoQ and 5.5% YoY.
  • IT services segment revenue was at $2,635.4 million, increase of 1.2% QoQ and 0.2% YoY.
  • Non-GAAP constant currency IT Services segment revenue increased 1.4% QoQ and decreased 1.2% YoY.
  • Total bookings was at $3,335 million, down 5.7% YoY in constant currency. Large deal bookings was at $871 million, decrease of 8.4% YoY in constant currency.
  • IT services operating margin for Q3’26 was 17.6%, expansion of 0.9% QoQ and 0.1% on YoY basis.
  • Net income for the quarter was at Rs 31.2 billion ($347.2 million), decrease of 3.9% QoQ and 7.0% YoY.
  • Earnings per share for the quarter at Rs 2.98 ($0.03), decrease of 3.9% QoQ and 7.2% YoY.
  • Adjusted for impact of labour code changes, Net Income for the quarter was Rs 33.6 billion ($374.3 million), increase of 3.6% QoQ and 0.3% YoY and EPS for the quarter was Rs 3.21 ($0.04), increase of 3.5 % QoQ and flat YoY.

  • Outlook for the Quarter ending March 31, 2026

    company expect revenue from our IT Services business segment to be in the range of $2,635 million to $2,688 million*. This translates to sequential guidance of 0% to 2.0% in constant currency terms.

data.title: U.S. earnings preview: High-quant financials and miners lead, while select defensives lag
data.description:
Alcoa (AA) leads the pack with a 4.92 quant rating, signaling strong momentum ahead of earnings, while Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) also ranks near the top at 4.79 on expectations of resilient demand and pricing. Financials dominate the high end, with Capital One (COF), Ally Financial (ALLY), Customers Bancorp (CUBI), Texas Capital Bancshares (TCBI) and KeyCorp (KEY) all scoring above 4.6. At the bottom, Booz Allen Hamilton (BAH) posts the weakest rating at 1.57, followed by Glacier Bancorp (GBCI), Bank OZK (OZK) and Cohen & Steers (CNS), reflecting softer factor trends ahead of results.

Overall, quant signals heading into the week point to stronger upside expectations in select financials and materials names, while investors appear more cautious on certain defensives and advisory-focused companies as earnings season gathers pace.

The list below ranks this week's upcoming earnings by their Seeking Alpha Quant ratings, which provide a comprehensive evaluation of each stock’s performance potential. These quantitative ratings incorporate factors such as valuation metrics, growth prospects, profitability, and momentum indicators.

Here are the top and bottom-rated upcoming earnings stocks, ranked by Quant:

Alcoa (AA), Quant Rating 4.92 - Strong Buy.

Capital One Financial (COF), Quant Rating 4.90 - Strong Buy.

Freeport-McMoRan (FCX), Quant Rating 4.79 - Strong Buy.

Ally Financial (ALLY), Quant Rating 4.76 - Strong Buy.

Customers Bancorp (CUBI), Quant Rating 4.68 - Strong Buy.

Texas Capital Bancshares (TCBI), Quant Rating 4.63 - Strong Buy.

KeyCorp (KEY), Quant Rating 4.63 - Strong Buy.

Associated Banc-Corp (ASB), Quant Rating 4.59 - Strong Buy.

Teck Resources (TECK), Quant Rating 4.46 - Buy.

Banc of California (BANC), Quant Rating 4.45 - Buy.

Alaska Air (ALK), Quant Rating 2.78 - Hold.

Fastenal Company (FAST), Quant Rating 2.70 - Hold.

McCormick & Company (MKC), Quant Rating 2.66 - Hold.

Old Republic International (ORI), Quant Rating 2.48 - Sell.

Simmons First National (SFNC), Quant Rating 2.43 - Sell.

Cathay General Bancorp (CATY), Quant Rating 2.40 - Sell.

Cohen & Steers (CNS), Quant Rating 2.34 - Sell.

Bank OZK (OZK), Quant Rating 2.30 - Hold.

Glacier Bancorp (GBCI), Quant Rating 2.23 - Sell.

Booz Allen Hamilton Holding (BAH), Quant Rating 1.57 - Sell.

data.title: Prospera announces $3M private placement
data.description:
• Prospera Energy (PEI:CA) announced a private placement of units for gross proceeds of $3M, at a price of $0.035 per unit.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share of the company and one common share purchase warrant, with each warrant entitling the holder to acquire one additional common share at an exercise price of $0.05 for a period of two years from the date of issuance, subject to customary acceleration provisions and standard anti-dilution adjustments.
  • The net proceeds from the offering, together with the cash flow, will be used to fund:
    • The Luseland Well Reactivation Program, focused on bringing previously shut-in heavy-oil wells back on production;
    • The Luseland Well Optimization Program, focused on installing additional recycle pumps and performing pump-upsize projects, including sand cleanouts, on currently active wells; and
    • The Cuthbert Workover Program, consisting of targeted workovers and optimization activities on shut-in wells to restore and improve production.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Marvion signs $200,000 stock purchase agreement
data.description:
• Marvion (MVNC) announced on Monday that it has entered into a stock purchase agreement with third-party investor Ray Mak Pak Fai.
  • Under the agreement, the investor agreed to purchase $200,000 worth of Marvion's common stock at a price of $0.0268 per share. The price reflects the average closing price of the company's common stock over the 15 trading days preceding December 15, 2025.
  • The firm said that the proceeds from this private placement will provide immediate capital to support ongoing operations, expand warehousing capacity, and fund strategic logistics initiatives across the company's subsidiaries.
  • The company intends to allocate the proceeds primarily toward strengthening operational resources and market expansion efforts.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Silver Elephant Mining announces $625,000 private placement
data.description:
• Silver Elephant Mining (ELEF:CA)  announced on Monday a non-brokered private placement  to raise gross proceeds of $625,000 through the sale of 2.5M units at a price of $0.25 per unit.
  • Each unit consists of one common share of the company and one share purchase warrant, with each warrant entitling the holder to purchase one additional share at a price of $0.32 per share for a period of three years from issuance.
  • Finder's fees of up to 7% finder's units will be payable, the company said. "Each finder's unit will consist of one share and one non-transferable share purchase warrant, with each warrant entitling the holder to purchase one additional share of the company at a price of $0.32 per share for three years."
  • Additionally, the firm stated that certain directors and officers of the company are expected to be subscribing for up to 550,000 units for gross proceeds of up to $137,500. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: MediPharm Labs appoints Greg Hunter as interim CEO
data.description:
• MediPharm Labs (LABS:CA) announced on Monday that David Pidduck will be stepping down from his role as the company's chief executive officer (CEO), effective January 2, 2026. 
  • The board of directors has appointed Greg Hunter, the company's chief financial officer, as interim CEO, while the firm evaluates its long‑term leadership plans. 
  • The company added that Hunter will continue to serve as chief financial officer during this interim period.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Millennial launches “bought deal” private placement for proceeds of $15.25M
data.description:
• Millennial Potash (MLP:CA) is conducting a “bought deal” private placement offering of 5M units of the company at a price of $3.05 per offered unit for aggregate gross proceeds of $15.25M.
  • Each offered unit will consist of one common share of the company and one-half of one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to acquire one common share at an exercise price of $4.00 at any time until the date that is three years from the closing date.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oakmark Select Fund Investor Class adds TRGP; exits CHTR and GLIBK among Q4 moves
data.description:
Oakmark Select Fund Investor Class outperformed the benchmark for the quarter, driven by strong performance in communication services and financials.

The fund, in its quarterly fund letter, stated that the industrials sector was the only detractor.

During the fourth quarter, the fund added Target Resources (TRGP), a midstream natural gas and natural gas liquids ((NGL)) company that is part of a group controlling 90% of the fractionation capacity in the world’s largest NGL hub, Mont Belvieu.

On the other hand, the fund announced final sales of Charter Communications Cl A (CHTR), GCI Liberty Cl C (GLIBK) and Liberty Broadband Cl C (LBRDK).

Source: Quarterly fund letter

data.title: Cathie Wood's weekly update: buys Broadcom, AMD; sells Tesla, Illumina
